summary: Fix #13269 : support multi-file import across different formats

description.text:
Closes #13269 

this PR improves the import command to properly handle importing multiple files with different supported formats in a single operation.

``ImportCommand::execute`` now calls ``dialogService.showFileOpenDialogAndGetMultipleFiles`` instead of ``dialogService.showFileOpenDialog`` and ``importSingleFile()`` has been refactored to ``importMultipleFiles()`` to handle one or more files

<!-- NOTE: If your work is not yet complete, please open a draft pull request. In that case, outline your intended next steps. Do you need feedback? Will you continue in parallel? ... -->

### Steps to test

1. File menu > Import > Import into current/new library
2. Select one or more files
3. ???
4. Profit

summary: fixes Tooltips are displayed for empty elements

description.text:
Closes #11681 

In about one to three sentences, describe the changes you have made: what, where, why, ...

I have made the change in ValueTableCellFactory.java in setOnMouseEntered event handler.
The issue demonstrated was that tooltips where visible on hovering beyond the entries list even in empty rows.
I have put a check that if the index of the current row is beyond the size of the items then tooltip is not required.

summary: If user moved file, it should simply be relinked - [Entry Editor part]

description.text:
A user might move files in the file directory without JabRef.

Then, JabRef does not find the file:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/125d342a-72cc-45ce-90d8-ecf0392c73a4)

But it still exists.

Example:

1. Open `test-support\src\manual-tests\issue-9798\issue-9798.bib in JabRef
2. Double click on the only entry to open the entry editor
3. Select tab "General"
4. See orange behind "minimal.pdf"\
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2adb3ef1-1530-4e03-b545-f4194ccc6212)

---

Normally `org.jabref.gui.fieldeditors.LinkedFilesEditorViewModel#findAssociatedNotLinkedFiles` is called (from org.jabref.gui.fieldeditors.LinkedFilesEditorViewModel#bindToEntry)

Task:

1. Write test in org.jabref.gui.externalfiles.AutoSetFileLinksUtilTest for this case
2. Adapt `org.jabref.gui.externalfiles.AutoSetFileLinksUtil` to cover the case searching for the filename (in addition to citation key logic).
3. Ensure that new logic ignores already linked files (e.g., if "minimal.pdf" is already attached to another entry)
4. Add test org.jabref.gui.externalfiles.AutoSetFileLinksUtilTest (if not already covered by 1)
5. Test in JabRef GUI

---

One has to ensure that "automatically search and show unlinked files" is activated

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0f63fcb9-ac3f-4ffb-b04f-c56ff0e20a64)

summary: Do not show "Merge PDF metadata" dialog if there is nothing to merge

description.text:
1. Create a new library
2. Drag'n'drop `jablib\build\resources\test\pdfs\minimal.pdf` into it
3. See Merge PDF metadata dialog appearing.
4. It first shows a "Grobid" column. \
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a914b110-a874-484c-a727-222662bfa794)
6. However, this disappears:\
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c2d5fc91-0156-417b-bfd3-eacc80548333)

Expected result: Dialog closes itself after if there is only one column shown (and the user did not do any manual changes)

---

This is a follow-up to https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/13132

It is Expection 1: "No dialog appears, because there is only one "real" source of information"

summary: New Entry: If DOI already exists in library offer "jump to entry"

description.text:
1. Create a new bib file
2. Add new entry for `https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cl.2018.01.004`\
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6abc06dc-8d42-4b93-9a47-5c84ad5a8bfe)
3. Add new entry for `https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cl.2018.01.004`

See that dialog is the same:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0039103f-36a2-47e6-b93c-5d6d5a7c9ef4)

Wish: Below the DOI identifier there is a hint displayed:

"Entry already existing in library. Select entry."

"Select entry." is a Hyperlink. It selectes the entry in the respective library.

Hints:

- `org.jabref.gui.LibraryTab#showAndEdit`can be used to jump to the entry

## Next step

After the initial functionality is made, one can implement it to handle more than the currently opened tab

See `org.jabref.gui.search.GlobalSearchResultDialog#initialize` at `resultsTable.setOnMouseClicked(event -> {` how to search an entry in any tab.

The hint should say: "Entry found in library X. Select entry."

Fixes https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/10842

summary: fix ClassCastException during drag-and-drop caused by transient tab state

description.text:
Closes #12921 


the type-check predicate can behave inconsistently during the interaction, likely a timing/synchronization issue caused by how the UI updates the tab list during drag-and-drop while the filtered list is reacting to changes.
directly mapping over the original tabs list with a lambda that accoutns for possible non-``LibraryTab`` entries solves the issue. this results in ``openDatabaseList`` possibly holding null values but since it is a private member not accessed elsewhere and we bind a filtered version to ``StateManager::openDatabases``, it should not cause any further bugs.

a more robust solution could be implemented with listeners, but it might be overkill ?

### Steps to test

1. Disable the setting to "Hide Tab bar when single library is present"
2. Create a library (Or create 2 if you skipped step 1)
3. Click and drag the Welcome tab and move it beside the newly created library
4. Exception no longer occurs

summary: Search results tab is blank after switching library

description.text:
Closes https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/13235

In about one to three sentences, describe the changes you have made: what, where, why, ...

Changes made to jabref.
Fix: Change "Add selected entries to database" to "Add selected entry(s) to library" in Citation Relations tab
This change follows the JabRef decision documented in:
https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/docs/decisions/0041-use-one-form-for-singular-and-plural.md  
and avoids confusion with the term "database", which is reserved for SQL databases.
